description: Create FINAL-REPORT.md - a comprehensive self-evaluation report of the Java + Kiro experiment based on the criteria in EXPERIMENT.md 'Future Evaluation Criteria' section
steps: Create /projects/sandbox/cdk-sleep-java-kiro/FINAL-REPORT.md with a comprehensive self-evaluation report

Structure the report with clear sections covering: Executive Summary, Code Quality Assessment, Process Quality Assessment, Agent Effectiveness Assessment, Language Suitability Assessment, Reproducibility Assessment, Quantitative Metrics, Qualitative Observations, Strengths, Weaknesses, and Conclusions

Under Code Quality: evaluate test coverage completeness (112 @Test methods across 15 files covering all 10 AWS resources), security posture (KMS, SSL, least-privilege IAM, block public access), error handling (granular Catch blocks, retry policies on all critical tasks, dual SNS notification), code organization (single stack, shared TestUtils, clear separation of concerns)

Under Process Quality: evaluate TDD adherence (test-first across 14 issues), commit history (conventional commits), architecture-code alignment (ARCHITECTURE.md matches implementation), documentation completeness (7 docs totaling 2149 lines)
Under Agent Effectiveness: evaluate issues completed (14 sequential), throughput (approximately one per day over 17 days), error recovery quality, scope discipline
Under Language Suitability: evaluate Java verbosity (545 lines of main source), jsii overhead and quirks encountered, Maven build reliability, type safety benefits, comparison notes
Under Reproducibility: evaluate whether meta-prompts are sufficient, documentation quality for handoff, maintenance considerations

Include honest self-assessment of weaknesses: no Lambda unit tests (Python index.py untested), no actual AWS deployment validation, snapshot test brittleness, Lambda testing boundary gap, some test methods use string-contains rather than structured assertions

Provide quantitative metrics table: 15 test files, 112 test methods, 545 LOC main Java, 234 LOC Python Lambda, 2242 LOC tests, 2149 LOC documentation, 10 AWS services, 14 issues, 17 day development period
Draw conclusions about the Java + Kiro combination's performance relative to the experiment goals
acceptance_criteria: Report evaluates all 5 criteria from EXPERIMENT.md Future Evaluation Criteria (Code Quality, Process Quality, Agent Effectiveness, Language Suitability, Reproducibility)
Report includes both quantitative metrics and qualitative observations
Report honestly identifies weaknesses and gaps (not just strengths)
Report draws actionable conclusions about the Java + Kiro combination
verification: Verify the file contains sections for all 5 evaluation criteria
Verify the file includes quantitative metrics
Verify the file includes an honest weaknesses/gaps section
findings: Report created at 458 lines covering all 5 evaluation criteria. Build/test verification skipped per context.json (Maven Central unreachable). Documentation-only change with no code behavior impact.
